content.full_text:
February 25, 1981

Ms. Barbara Myers  
County Clerk Election Department  
Post Office Box 431  
Riverside, California 92502

Dear Barbara:

Thank you very much for your letter dated February 18, 1981 regarding District Directors and their filing obligation under the Political Reform Act.

The facts, as I understand them are:

1) The District Directors referred in your letter are not being voted upon in 1981;
2) No District Director maintains a campaign fund or controlled committee during "off year;"
3) No District Director raises contributions or makes any expenditures in connection with his or her own campaign during "off year;" and,
4) The Directors often make sizable contributions from their own personal funds to candidates for other public offices.

The question you raised is if such contribution(s) reached $500 by March 17, would the Director making the contribution(s) be required to file the Threshold Statements due by March 22?

Government Code Section 84200(b) in part states that, "elected officers ... that have made contributions or independent expenditures of $500 or more during the threshold periods specified below shall file campaign statements on the following dates: (1) No later than March 22, with a $500 threshold period of March 1 through March 17 ..."

Therefore, since the contribution(s) by the Director meets the threshold statement level by virtue of being $500, Threshold Statement shall be filed by the Director for the period ending March 1 and ending March 17, 1981.
